news.t: European stocks are poised to outdo their American peers. These catalysts explain why.
Eurozone Economic Sentiment Inches Higher After Middle East Tensions Ease
Eurozone Household Inflation Expectations Cooled in May
STOXX 600 opens flat as investors weigh US-Iran talks and ECB outlook
Europe must rethink medicine pricing or risk losing investments, pharma lobby chief says
Eurozone Consumer Confidence Picks Up Slightly
Eastern and central European countries demand EU strengthens carbon fund for poorer members
Europe swelters under heatwave, prompting crisis talks in France
European leaders are grappling with how to stem a flood of Chinese exports hammering their industries—and whether they are ready for a trade war with Beijing over the problem
Europe's mid-sized inflation shock requires measured response, ECB's Lane says

holdings.infoBox: DBEF is an equity ETF with a total of 720 individual holdings. The top holdings are Currency Contract - Usd stock at 91.97%, ASML Holding at 3.02%, Dws Govt Money Market Series Institutional Shares at 1.85%, HSBC Holdings at 1.41%, and Roche Holding AG at 1.25%.

overview.description: The Xtrackers MSCI EAFE Hedged Equity ETF (DBEF) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in total market equity. The fund tracks an index of developed-market equities excluding North America. It is hedged for currency exposure from a USD point of view. DBEF was launched on Jun 9, 2011 and is issued by Xtrackers.
infoTable: MSCI EAFE 100% Hedged to USD Net Variant

data.title: European stocks are poised to outdo their American peers. These catalysts explain why.
data.text: JPMorgan raises its year-end target for European stocks again. Panmure warns that high valuations imply negative returns for the U.S. in the next decade.

data.title: Eurozone Economic Sentiment Inches Higher After Middle East Tensions Ease
data.text: The European Commission said its economic sentiment gauge rose, driven by improving confidence in almost all sectors surveyed.

data.title: Eurozone Household Inflation Expectations Cooled in May
data.text: Inflation expectations for the year ahead among households in the eurozone fell, as consumers reacted to easing tensions in the Middle East, likely reassuring ECB policymakers.

data.title: Eurozone Consumer Confidence Picks Up Slightly
data.text: The European Commission's flash consumer-confidence indicator rose to minus 17.7 in June, continuing its tentative recovery from a three-year low in April.

data.text: The European Union is set to remove barriers preventing banks from moving funds across ​the bloc, the Financial Times reported on Thursday, ‌citing a draft European Commission report.
